data.title: Tiny Dreamers Studio Recognized as Top Photo Studio in Greater Sacramento
data.description:
Sacramento, California--(Newsfile Corp. - June 4, 2025) - Tiny Dreamers Studio, a leading newborn and family photography studio, has been named one of the best photo studios in the Greater Sacramento area by BestPhotoStudios.com. This recognition highlights the studio's commitment to capturing precious moments and delivering exceptional quality to its clients.

Image 1

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/10373/253388_84301b2ca4ef7bc2_002full.jpg

Image 2

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/10373/253388_84301b2ca4ef7bc2_003full.jpg

BestPhotoStudios.com, a respected authority in the photography industry, conducted an extensive review of photo studios in the region. Tiny Dreamers Studio stood out for its innovative techniques, attention to detail, and ability to create timeless memories for families.

"We are thrilled to be recognized as one of the top photo studios in Greater Sacramento," said Tiny Dreamers Studio founder Bree Franklin. "This acknowledgment is a testament to our team's dedication and passion for capturing the most cherished moments in our clients' lives."

Image 3

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/10373/253388_84301b2ca4ef7bc2_004full.jpg

The studio's specialization in newborn and family photography has earned it a loyal following among local families. Tiny Dreamers Studio is known for its safe and comfortable environment, state-of-the-art equipment, and creative approach to preserving life's milestones.

This recognition comes as Tiny Dreamers Studio celebrates over 10 years of service to the Greater Sacramento community. The studio has photographed hundreds of newborns and families, creating lasting memories for countless households in the area.

Image 4

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/10373/253388_84301b2ca4ef7bc2_005full.jpg

Looking ahead, Tiny Dreamers Studio plans to expand its services and continue innovating in newborn and family photography. The studio is committed to maintaining its high standards and pushing the boundaries of creativity in capturing life's precious moments.

For more information about Tiny Dreamers Studio and its services, please visit https://tinydreamersstudio.com/ or contact Bree Franklin at bree@tinydreamersstudio.com.

About Tiny Dreamers Studio:

Tiny Dreamers Studio is a premier newborn and family photography studio in Greater Sacramento, California. Founded in 2010, the studio creates timeless, high-quality images that capture the essence of family bonds and the wonder of new life. Focusing on safety, creativity, and client satisfaction, Tiny Dreamers Studio has become a trusted name in preserving precious memories for families throughout the Greater Sacramento area.

data.title: Trisura Group Outperform Rating, C$57.00 Price Target, Confirmed at National Bank of Canada
data.description:
National Bank of Canada on Tuesday maintained its outperform rating and C$57.00 price target on the shares of Trisura Group following the specialty insurer's 2025 investor day.

Trisura Group held its investor day on Tuesday.

The bank noted that the company's management team presented a confident outlook for the business.

The bank said it is reiterating its favorable view that the company will deliver "clean and profitable quarters" in the immediate term, as the company's management reiterated financial objectives, highlighted "the ample financial flexibility to fund growth aspirations and outlined a positive view for growth and profitability in all business lines."

data.title: Johnson Fistel, PLLP Investigates Abacus Global on Behalf of Shareholders Who Incurred Losses
data.description:
SAN DIEGO, June 04, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Johnson Fistel, PLLP is investigating whether Abacus Global Management, Inc. or any of its executive officers or others violated securities laws by misrepresenting or failing to timely disclose information to investors.

What if I purchased Abacus Global securities? If you purchased securities and suffered losses on your investment, join our investigation now: Click Here to Join the Investigation

For more information, contact Jim Baker at jimb@johnsonfistel.com or (619) 814-4471

There is no cost or obligation to you.

What is Johnson Fistel investigating?  Morpheus Research recently published a detailed report on Abacus Global that called into question the integrity of the company’s asset valuation methods. Based on a three-month investigation involving interviews with former employees, analysis of internal documents and footnotes from financial statements, the report argues that Abacus Global’s seemingly stellar returns may not stem from genuine investment performance but rather from an opaque accounting scheme. According to Morpheus, a reliance on ever-shifting fair-value estimates and related-party transactions has inflated reported profits and hidden underlying risks, raising fresh concerns among investors about whether Abacus Global’s books truly reflect its economic reality.

Johnson Fistel, PLLP is a nationally recognized shareholder rights law firm with offices in California, New York, Georgia, Idaho, and Colorado. The firm represents individual and institutional investors in shareholder derivative and securities class action lawsuits. We also extend our services to foreign investors who have purchased on US exchanges. Stay updated with news on stock drops and learn how Johnson Fistel, PLLP can help you recover your losses. For more information about the firm and its attorneys, please visit http://www.johnsonfistel.com.

Achievements: In 2024, Johnson Fistel was honored to be ranked in the Top 10 Plaintiff Law Firms by the ISS Securities Class Action Services. This recognition underscores our effectiveness in advocating for investors, having recovered approximately $90,725,000 for aggrieved clients in cases where we served as lead or co-lead counsel. data.title: Italy Issues 17 Bln Euros in Dual-Tranche Syndicated Transaction — Update
data.description:
By Emese Bartha

Italy issued 17 billion euros ($19.33 billion) in a dual-tranche syndicated government bond transaction on Wednesday, one of the lead manager banks said.

The transaction included the launch of a new October 2030-dated government bond, or BTP, in which Italy placed 12 billion euros, the same bank said. Books for this leg of the deal closed in excess of 120 billion euros, including 5.3 billion euros in joint lead manager interest.

The spread on this bond was set at 8 basis points above the yield of the 2.95% July 2030 BTP. The new October 2030 BTP has a 2.70% coupon, and it was priced at 99.761, equivalent to a 2.768% gross annual yield, Italy's Ministry of Economy and Finance said.

The other leg of the transaction was a 5 billion-euro tap of the 4.05% October 2037 green BTP, which attracted more than 90 billion euros in demand, the ministry said. This book size is slightly smaller than the one indicated by banks at book close.

The spread on this tap was set at 6 basis points above the yield of the 0.95% March 2037 BTP. The bond was priced at 103.866, implying a yield of 3.692%, the ministry said.

Joint lead managers of the transaction were Banco Santander SA, Barclays Bank Ireland PLC, BNP Paribas, BofA Securities Europe S.A., Credit Agricole Corp. Inv. Bank and Societe Generale Inv. Banking.

Write to Emese Bartha at emese.bartha@wsj.com

data.title: Oil Falls on Oversupply Concerns
data.description:
WTI crude oil futures dropped over 1% to below $63 per barrel as Saudi Arabia signaled it may push for a large production increase, raising fears of a global oil oversupply.

The kingdom reportedly wants OPEC+ to boost output by at least 411,000 barrels per day in August, and possibly in September, aiming to capture market share during peak summer demand.

This comes after a July production increase was announced last weekend, though it was within expectations.

Last month, Saudi Arabia led the rise in OPEC output but still held back from adding its full quota.

Meanwhile, US crude inventories fell by 4.3 million barrels last week, hinting at tighter short-term supply, though gasoline demand also dipped.

Traders remain cautious amid trade tensions, with President Trump calling China’s leader “extremely hard” to strike a deal with, clouding the outlook for global demand.

data.title: SBM Offshore signs Share Purchase Agreement with GEPetrol
data.description:
Amsterdam, June 4, 2025

SBM Offshore announces it has signed a Share Purchase Agreement for the full divestment of SBM Offshore’s equity interest in the lease and operating entities of the FPSO Aseng to GEPetrol. The Company’s exit from Equatorial Guinea will take place following an operational transition phase lasting up to 12 months.

SBM Offshore’s sale of its participation in the unit in Equatorial Guinea is in line with its strategy to rationalize its Lease & Operate portfolio, as per other recent transactions.

The agreement remains subject to several conditions precedent and approvals.

Corporate Profile

SBM Offshore is the world’s deepwater ocean-infrastructure expert. Through the design, construction, installation, and operation of offshore floating facilities, we play a pivotal role in a just transition. By advancing our core, we deliver cleaner, more efficient energy production. By pioneering more, we unlock new markets within the blue economy. 

More than 7,800 SBMers collaborate worldwide to deliver innovative solutions as a responsible partner towards a sustainable future, balancing ocean protection with progress.

For further information, please visit our website at www.sbmoffshore.com.

data.title: Brent Crude Oil Falls Below $65
data.description:
Brent crude oil futures dropped over 1% to below $65 per barrel as Saudi Arabia signaled it may push for a large production increase, raising fears of a global oil oversupply.

The kingdom reportedly wants OPEC+ to boost output by at least 411,000 barrels per day in August, and possibly in September, aiming to capture market share during peak summer demand.

This comes after a July production increase was announced last weekend, though it was within expectations.

Last month, Saudi Arabia led the rise in OPEC output but still held back from adding its full quota.

Meanwhile, US crude inventories fell by 4.3 million barrels last week, hinting at tighter short-term supply, though gasoline demand also dipped.

Traders remain cautious amid trade tensions, with President Trump calling China’s leader “extremely hard” to strike a deal with, clouding the outlook for global demand.

data.title: Cap-free Wells Fargo could be a fashion faux pas
data.description:
Despite a years-long $2 trln asset limit, the US lender’s stock outperformed most peers since 2020. Forced efficiency amid brutal rate hikes was a boon as scandal rocked the C-suite. Now that regulators are freeing it to chase growth, the risk is that it blends in with the crowd.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Stephen Gandel on X.

CONTEXT NEWS

Wells Fargo announced on June 3 that the Federal Reserve had removed limits on its growth, first imposed in 2018, that capped its total assets at $1.95 trillion. The cap was imposed following revelations that the bank’s employees had fraudulently opened accounts for clients without their knowledge.

data.title: Element 29 Renews 5-Year Surface Access Agreement at Elida Project
data.description:
Element 29 Resources on Wednesday signed a renewed five-year surface-access agreement with the host community at its flagship Elida Porphyry Copper-Molybdenum-Silver (Cu-Mo-Ag) project in central Peru.

This agreement, which runs to the end of April, 2030, secures long-term access to support both current and future exploration activities. Element 29 Resources said this agreement was a "key requirement" for its recent application for a new drill permit under an expanded and more comprehensive Declaracion de Impacto Ambiental (DIA) environmental approval.

The company intends to use the magnetotelluric data to help delineate deeper, higher-grade Cu-Mo-Ag mineralization and to guide exploration of the broader hydrothermal alteration footprint of the Elida deposit below 500m. It also intends to employ community members to assist with the continuation of the Elida Phase-III drilling program under the existing Ficha Tecnica Ambiental (FTA) environmental approval and drill permit.

Element 29 added that it is preparing a Collective Impacts Report to support its request for exemption from the Consulta Previa process, which is required as part of the new drill permit application under the DIA environmental approval.

The company's shares were last seen up $0.01 to $0.53 on the TSX Venture Exchange.

data.title: Canada to Hold Off on Retaliating in Response to Higher U.S. Metals Tariffs
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--Canada is going to wait before enacting any further retaliation against the U.S. over higher steel and aluminum tariffs because negotiators representing Ottawa and Washington are in talks on a new bilateral economic-and-security deal, Prime Minister Mark Carney said.

President Trump on Tuesday formally signed an order that would double the tariff on foreign-made steel and aluminum to 50%. Canada is America's largest supplier of both metals.

Carney said the U.S.'s doubling of steel and aluminum tariffs was illegal and unjustified, and added that Canada already has in place 25% tariffs on about $43.7 billion of U.S. imports. Ottawa would take its time before deciding its next move, the prime minister said, "because we are in intensive discussions right now with the Americans."

"Those discussions are progressing," he added.

Carney also said Trump's decision affects all exporters of steel, not just Canada.

The Aluminum Association of Canada said the 50% tariff would suppress demand for the metal across North America, and affect sectors such as autos, defense and construction.

The Canadian Steel Producers Association has called on the Liberal government to impose 50% tariffs on imported U.S. steel and added that the consequences from the U.S.'s higher tariff would be unrecoverable for the economy.

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

data.title: Investigation Alert: Photronics, Inc. (PLAB) Under Scrutiny - Contact Levi & Korsinsky for Details
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / June 4, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Photronics, Inc. ("Photronics, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

Photronics issued a press release on May 28, 2025, reporting its financial results for the second quarter of 2025. Among other items, Photronics reported revenue of $210.99 million, representing a 2.8% year-over-year decline and missing consensus estimates by $1.01 million, and non-GAAP earnings per share of $0.40, missing consensus estimates by $0.08. Photronics also issued third quarter revenue guidance in the range of $200 million to $208 million, compared to consensus estimates of $220 million. In the same press release, Photronics announced the resignation of Dr. Frank Lee from his role as the Company's Chief Executive Office, effective immediately.

Following this news, Photronics' stock price fell over 15% on the same day. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/photronics-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=151658&wire=1&utm_campaign=33

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. data.title: Exchange-Traded Funds Mixed, US Equities Rise After Midday
data.description:
Broad Market Indicators

Broad-market exchange-traded fund IWM declined and IVV rose. Actively traded Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) was up 0.3%.

US equity indexes rose in midday trading Wednesday following an increase in the probability of interest-rate cuts later this year after a services index signaled contraction.

Energy

iShares US Energy ETF (IYE) and the Energy Select Sector SPDR (XLE) each shed about 1.6%.

Technology

Technology Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLK) added 0.1%; iShares US Technology ETF (IYW) and iShares Expanded Tech Sector ETF (IGM) advanced.

SPDR S&P Semiconductor (XSD) climbed 2.1%, and iShares Semiconductor (SOXX) rose 1.5%.

Financial

Financial Select Sector SPDR (XLF) dipped 0.2%. Direxion Daily Financial Bull 3X Shares (FAS) fell 0.5%, and its bearish counterpart, Direxion Daily Financial Bear 3X Shares (FAZ), rose 0.6%.

Commodities

Crude oil fell 1.1%, and the United States Oil Fund (USO) was down by 1%. Natural gas dipped 0.5%, and the United States Natural Gas Fund (UNG) was 0.8% lower.

Gold was up 0.7% on Comex, and SPDR Gold Shares (GLD) rose 0.6%. Silver lost 0.1%, and iShares Silver Trust (SLV) dropped 0.4%.

Consumer

Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR (XLP) slipped 0.1%; Vanguard Consumer Staples ETF (VDC) and the iShares Dow Jones US Consumer Goods (IYK) were mixed, with the latter rising 0.1%.

Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR (XLY) lost less than 0.1%; VanEck Retail ETF (RTH) was down fractionally, while SPDR S&P Retail (XRT) fell 0.4%.

Health Care

Health Care Select Sector SPDR (XLV) rose 0.8%. iShares US Healthcare (IYH) and Vanguard Health Care ETF (VHT) also edged higher. iShares Biotechnology ETF (IBB) added 1%.

Industrial

Industrial Select Sector SPDR (XLI) was up 0.4%. Vanguard Industrials (VIS) and iShares US Industrials (IYJ) rose as well.

data.title: Corn bids mixed, soy higher as farmers hesitate to sell
data.description:
Spot soybean bids were steady to stronger while corn bids were mixed in the U.S. Midwest on Wednesday as farmers remained reluctant to book sales amid relatively low prices, dealers said.

  • Soybean bids ticked up at an Ohio elevator and an Iowa river terminal.

  • Corn bids rose at an Indiana ethanol plant and a Nebraska processor. Bids fell at river terminals in Illinois and Iowa.

  • Favorable conditions for U.S. crops remained a curb on prices, which hit a six-month low for corn on Tuesday and a seven-week low for soybeans on Monday.

  • Corn planting is nearly completed while soy seeding is drawing to a close.

  • Soybean futures were last up 9 cents to $10.49-3/4 per bushel. Corn futures were up 8-1/4 cents to $10.42-1/2 per bushel

data.title: Mortgage Applications Decline as Potential Borrowers Await Larger Rate Drops
data.description:
US mortgage applications declined for the third week in a row as potential borrowers waited for loan rates to fall more substantially, the Mortgage Bankers Association said Wednesday.

The market composite index, which measures loan application volume, dropped 3.9% on a seasonally adjusted basis for the week ended Friday. Without adjustments, the index slid 15% on a weekly basis.

The refinance and purchase indexes fell 4% each from the previous week.

"Mortgage applications decreased over the week but continue to exhibit annual gains, with purchase applications running 18% ahead of last year's place," said Joel Kan, the association's deputy chief economist. "Refinance activity fell across both conventional and government segment and the overall average refinance loan size was the smallest since July 2024, as potential borrowers hold out for larger rate drops."

The 30-year rate on mortgages with conforming loan balances of $806,500 or less dropped to 6.92% from 6.98% a week ago, according to the MBA. For loan balances higher than that amount, the rate eased to 6.92% from 6.93%. For 15-year loans, the rate ticked up to 6.25% from 6.23%.

Last week, the National Association of Realtors said pending home sales fell more than projected in April as elevated mortgage rates dampened buyer enthusiasm.

On Monday, real-estate brokerage Redfin (RDFN) said the total value of US home listings hit a record high in April amid increasing inventory, cooling home-buying demand and rising sale prices.
